questions.stem:
-- I have never _____ to Beijing before.
-- Really? I will take you there next summer.
questions.explanation: have been to 表示'去过某地（已回来）'；have gone to 表示'去了某地（未回）'。这里说话人和对方正在对话，说明人在现场，应用 have been to。went 是过去时不能和 have 搭配。

questions.stem: My sister _____ the book for two weeks. She should return it to the library.
questions.explanation: for two weeks 表示持续一段时间，需要延续性动词。borrow 是非延续性动词，不能和 for 连用，其延续性替换词是 keep。lend 也是非延续性动词。所以选 has kept。记住：borrow→keep, buy→have, join→be in。
宾语从句三要素：(1) 引导词 what；(2) 陈述语序（主+谓），排除A、C；(3) 主句 asked 是过去时，从句要用过去时态呼应，排除D。所以选B：what they were doing。

questions.stem: I wonder _____ he will come to the party or not.
questions.explanation: 表示'是否'时 if 和 whether 都可用，但当后面有 or not 紧跟时，只能用 whether，不能用 if。that 引导确定的陈述句，what 引导缺成分的从句，都不合适。

questions.stem: The girl _____ is standing over there is my classmate Lucy.
questions.explanation: 定语从句修饰先行词 the girl（人），关系代词在从句中作主语（is standing），所以用 who。which 修饰物；what 不引导定语从句；whom 作宾语。

questions.stem: It was such an _____ (excite) match that all the fans stood up and cheered loudly.
questions.explanation: -ing 形容词修饰物（令人兴奋的比赛）；-ed 形容词修饰人（感到兴奋的）。这里修饰 match（比赛），所以用 exciting。excitement 是名词；excitedly 是副词。

questions.stem: We should _____ measures to protect the environment before it is too late.
questions.explanation: take measures 是固定搭配，意思是'采取措施'。类似搭配：take action（采取行动）、take steps（采取步骤）。make、do、give 都不与 measures 搭配。

questions.stem: The scientist devoted all his life to the _____ of new medicines to help sick people.
questions.explanation: 根据语境，科学家毕生致力于新药物的'研发/开发'（development）。agreement（协议）、environment（环境）、entertainment（娱乐）均不符合语境。devote...to 表示'致力于'。

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a special programme called 'Buddy Reading'. The idea was simple: older students would spend time reading with younger ones during lunch break.

At first, I felt it was a waste of time. I was already (9)_____ with my own schoolwork. Why should I spend my free time helping little kids? But my English teacher said it would be a meaningful (10)_____.

On the first day, I met a boy named Tim. He was very (11)_____ and did not say a word. I picked up a picture book and started reading aloud. Slowly, Tim moved closer and pointed at the colourful pictures.

Over the next few weeks, something amazing happened. Tim began to read short sentences by himself. His eyes would (12)_____ every time he finished a page. I realised that I was not just teaching him -- he was teaching me too. He taught me that the greatest joy comes from (13)_____ others grow.
questions.explanation: 根据后文'Why should I spend my free time helping little kids?'可知'我'觉得自己课业已经很忙了，没有空余时间，所以选 busy（忙碌的）。bored（无聊的）、satisfied（满意的）、angry（生气的）不符合语境。
老师说这会是一段有意义的'经历'（experience）。discovery（发现）、mistake（错误）、competition（比赛）不合语境。a meaningful experience 是常见搭配。
后文说'did not say a word'（一个字都没说），说明 Tim 很害羞（shy）。excited（兴奋的）、naughty（淘气的）、clever（聪明的）与'一个字没说'的描述不匹配。
Tim 每次读完一页都很高兴，眼睛会'亮起来'（light up）。light up 表示眼睛发光、面露喜色，是叙事文中描写喜悦的常用搭配。close（关上）、turn around（转身）、look down（向下看）不符合。
最大的快乐来自于'看着'（watching）别人成长。watch sb grow 是合理搭配，表示见证成长。forcing（强迫）、preventing（阻止）、leaving（离开）都不符合文章主题——帮助他人成长的喜悦。

questions.group_passage:
Have you ever heard of 'plogging'? It is a fitness trend that started in Sweden in 2016. The word comes from the Swedish 'plocka upp' (pick up) and 'jogging'. When people go plogging, they jog and pick up litter at the same time.

Plogging is more than just running. Because ploggers bend down, squat, and stretch to pick up rubbish, they actually burn more calories than regular joggers. A study shows that ploggers can burn about 288 calories in 30 minutes, while normal joggers burn only about 235.

The trend has spread to more than 100 countries. In some cities, plogging groups meet every weekend. They bring reusable bags and gloves, then run through parks, streets, and beaches. After each run, they sort the rubbish into recycling and non-recycling.

Experts say plogging is popular because it combines personal health benefits with community improvement. People feel good about exercising AND making their neighbourhood cleaner. Some schools have even started 'plogging clubs' to encourage students to stay active while caring for the environment.

However, some critics point out that plogging alone cannot solve the waste problem. They believe governments should focus more on reducing plastic production and improving waste management systems. Still, supporters argue that plogging raises awareness and inspires people to think twice before littering.
questions.stem: According to the passage, how many calories can ploggers burn in 30 minutes?
questions.explanation: 原文第二段明确说'ploggers can burn about 288 calories in 30 minutes'。235是普通跑步者的消耗量。做细节题要精准定位原文数据，注意区分不同对象的数字。

questions.stem: We can infer from the passage that plogging _____ .
options: A. was invented by a famous athlete
B. is only suitable for young people
C. is good for both health and the environment
D. will completely solve the litter problem
questions.explanation: 文章多处提到 plogging 既锻炼身体（burn more calories）又清洁环境（pick up litter/making neighbourhood cleaner），可以推断它对健康和环境都有好处。A文中未提及运动员发明；B没说只适合年轻人；D最后一段指出它不能单独解决垃圾问题。

questions.stem: The underlined word 'awareness' in the last paragraph probably means _____ .
questions.explanation: 原文说'plogging raises awareness and inspires people to think twice before littering'，即 plogging 提高了人们的意识，让人们乱扔垃圾前三思。awareness 在这里指对环境问题的'认知和关注'（knowledge and attention）。

questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: B. Plogging: Running for a Cleaner World
D. Why We Should Stop Using Plastic
questions.explanation: 全文围绕 plogging（捡垃圾式跑步）这一活动展开，介绍了它的起源、好处和影响。B选项'Plogging: Running for a Cleaner World'既点出了核心概念又概括了主旨。A只涉及跑步；C以偏概全；D只是文末提到的一个观点。主旨题技巧：找能涵盖全文的选项。

questions.group_passage:
Last Saturday, our class went to Green Hills to plant trees. (18)_____ We took a bus and arrived at 9 a.m.

Our teacher divided us into groups of four. (19)_____ Some carried water, and others put young trees into the holes carefully.

By noon, we had planted over 50 trees. (20)_____ We took photos together and promised to come back to water the trees every month.

(21)_____ It was tiring but meaningful. I hope the trees will grow tall and provide shade for people in the future.
options: A. Each group had a different job.
B. Everyone felt proud and happy.
C. The weather was sunny and warm.
D. On our way home, we sang songs on the bus.
E. Some students dug holes in the ground.
questions.explanation: 第(18)空在首段，前面说去种树，后面说坐车9点到达。这里需要一句过渡，描述当天的天气情况，'The weather was sunny and warm'最合适，为户外活动做铺垫。

options: A. Each group had a different job.
B. Everyone felt proud and happy.
C. The weather was sunny and warm.
D. On our way home, we sang songs on the bus.
E. Some students dug holes in the ground.
questions.explanation: 第(19)空前面说分成四人小组，后面说'Some carried water, and others put young trees into holes'，都是在描述各人的具体工作。E'Some students dug holes in the ground'（挖洞）也是种树的一个步骤，与后文的搬水、放树形成并列。

options: A. Each group had a different job.
B. Everyone felt proud and happy.
C. The weather was sunny and warm.
D. On our way home, we sang songs on the bus.
E. Some students dug holes in the ground.
questions.explanation: 第(20)空前面说到中午种了50多棵树，后面说合影并约定每月回来浇水。种完树后大家'感到自豪和开心'（Everyone felt proud and happy）最符合逻辑，承上启下。

options: A. Each group had a different job.
B. Everyone felt proud and happy.
C. The weather was sunny and warm.
D. On our way home, we sang songs on the bus.
E. Some students dug holes in the ground.
questions.explanation: 第(21)空在末段开头，后面说'It was tiring but meaningful'是总结感想。D'On our way home, we sang songs on the bus'描述回家路上的情景，是活动结束到总结之间的自然过渡。干扰项A（Each group had a different job）放这里不合逻辑。
